diagrams.title: 0/1 Knapsack DP Table Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the 2D DP table structure with items as rows and capacity as columns
elements: Arrows showing transition from dp[i-1][w] and dp[i-1][w-weight[i]]
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how DP table is filled and recurrence works

diagrams.title: Selection Reconstruction Path
diagrams.description: Shows how to trace back from dp[n][W] to find selected items
elements: If dp[i][w] ≠ dp[i-1][w], arrow goes to dp[i-1][w-weight[i]] (item selected)
If dp[i][w] = dp[i-1][w], arrow goes to dp[i-1][w] (item not selected)
Highlight the path of selected items
diagrams.purpose: Learning to reconstruct the solution and identify which items were chosen

diagrams.title: 0/1 vs Unbounded Knapsack
diagrams.description: Compares iteration patterns for 0/1 and Unbounded Knapsack
elements: Shows how same item can be used multiple times in unbounded
diagrams.purpose: Distinguishing between the two variants and understanding iteration direction
